curriculum.subject: Mathematics
curriculum.overallDescription: 6 units, 15 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

units.description: Introduction to Polynomials, Sequences and Progressions, Exploring Algebraic Identities, Linear Equations in Two Variables
Axioms and Postulates, Lines and Angles, Triangles – Congruence Theorems, 4-gons (Quadrilaterals), Circles
Area and Perimeter, Surface Area and Volume

key_concepts: Understanding of numbers including natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers
Properties of the set of real numbers
Relationships amongst numbers and number sets
Understanding of powers, radical powers, and exponents
Recursion relations for Virahanka numbers
Discovery and proof of algebraic identities
Remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm
Specifying locations and describing spatial relationships
Proving mathematical statements using stated assumptions, axioms, postulates
Congruence of two-dimensional geometric shapes
Proving theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for triangles
Proving theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for quadrilaterals
Geometry of a circle including chords, subtended angles, inscribed polygons
Calculating area of a triangle using Heron's formula
Calculating surface areas and volumes of solid objects
Measures of central tendency: mean, median, and mode
Concepts from probability for likelihood of everyday events

practicals.title: Lab Practical Assessment
practicals.details: Assessment component worth 5 marks involving lab activities from prescribed books, focusing on graphical representation of data, geometric constructions (bisectors, triangles), and calculation of surface areas/volumes.

activities.title: Construct the Square Root Spiral
activities.details: Students will construct a square root spiral to visualize irrational numbers.

activities.title: Visualise Algebraic Identities using Geometrical Models
activities.details: Students will use geometric models and algebra tiles to visualise identities, factorisation of quadratic expressions, and simplification of rational expressions.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Students will solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to understand recursive rules in sequences.

activities.title: Represent a Floor Plan on a Grid using Coordinates
activities.details: Students will specify locations and positions of points relative to each other using coordinates.

activities.title: Constructing a Square with a Given Side (Sulbasutras)
activities.details: Students will describe the construction of a square as given in Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

activities.title: Drawing, Measurement, and Paper Manipulation Activities for 4-gons
activities.details: Students will engage in drawing, measurement, and paper manipulation to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and quadrilaterals.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions using Axioms
activities.details: Students will construct bisectors of line segments, angles, triangles, and other polygons satisfying given constraints.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle (Baudhayana's Sulbasutras)
activities.details: Students will explore the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape using methods from ancient civilisations.

activities.title: Empirical Probability Experiments
activities.details: Students will perform experiments to estimate the empirical probability of events.

projects.title: Mathematics Laboratory Activities (Prescribed Books)
projects.details: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books: 'Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX' and 'Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage'. Includes graphing data, constructing geometric shapes, and measuring 3D objects.

marks_distribution: Unit VI: Statistics and Probability - 10 Marks

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units with emphasis on logical reasoning, problem-solving, mathematical modelling, and computational thinking as per NCF-SE 2023.
CG-1 to CG-11 mapped to specific chapters (e.g., Number System maps to CG-1; Algebra maps to CG-3).
internal_assessment: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment (5+5): 10 Marks

practical_assessment.description: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
source_materials: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)

competency_groups.title: Understands numbers and number sets
competency_groups.description: Understands numbers (natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real), ways of representing numbers, relationships amongst numbers, and number sets.
competencies: C-1.1 Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.

competency_groups.title: Discovers algebraic identities and models real-life situations
competency_groups.description: Discovers and proves algebraic identities and models real-life situations in the form of equations to solve them.
competencies: C-3.1 States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm.

C-3.2 Models and solves contextualised problems using equations (for example, simultaneous linear equations in two variables or single polynomial equations), and draws conclusions about a situation being modelled.
C-3.3 Learns Brahmagupta's quadratic formula (in both symbolic and poetic form) and its derivation, and uses it to solve some of the poetic puzzles of Bhaskara as well as modern-day problems.

competency_groups.title: Discovers algebraic identities and models real-life situations
competency_groups.description: Discovers and proves algebraic identities and models real-life situations in the form of equations to solve them.
competencies: C-3.1 States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm.

C-3.2 Models and solves contextualised problems using equations (for example, simultaneous linear equations in two variables or single polynomial equations), and draws conclusions about a situation being modelled.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics of geometric shapes and develops arguments
competency_groups.description: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes, and develops mathematical arguments to explain geometric relationships.
competencies: C-4.1 Describes relationships including congruence of two-dimensional geometric shapes (such as lines, angles, triangles) to make and test conjectures and solve problems.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics of geometric shapes and develops arguments
competency_groups.description: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes, and develops mathematical arguments to explain geometric relationships.
competencies: C-4.2 Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for triangles and quadrilaterals, and applies them to solve geometric problems.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics of geometric shapes and develops arguments
competency_groups.description: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes, and develops mathematical arguments to explain geometric relationships.
competencies: C-4.2 Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for triangles and quadrilaterals, and applies them to solve geometric problems.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics of geometric shapes and develops arguments
competency_groups.description: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes, and develops mathematical arguments to explain geometric relationships.
competencies: C-4.3 Proves theorems about the geometry of a circle, including its chords, subtended angles, inscribed polygons, and area in terms of pi.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics of geometric shapes and develops arguments
competency_groups.description: Analyses characteristics and properties of two-dimensional geometric shapes, and develops mathematical arguments to explain geometric relationships.
competencies: C-4.5 Specifies locations and describes spatial relationships using coordinate geometry.

competency_groups.title: Derives and uses formulae for areas, surface area, and volumes
competency_groups.description: Derives and uses formulae to calculate areas of plane figures, surface area, and volumes of solid objects.
competencies: C-5.1 Visualises, represents, and calculates the area of a triangle using Heron's formula and its generalisation to cyclic quadrilaterals given by Brahmagupta's formula.

competency_groups.title: Derives and uses formulae for areas, surface area, and volumes
competency_groups.description: Derives and uses formulae to calculate areas of plane figures, surface area, and volumes of solid objects.
competencies: C-5.2 Visualises and uses mathematical thinking to discover formulae to calculate surface areas and volumes of solid objects (cubes, cuboids, spheres, hemispheres, right circular cylinders or cones, and their combinations).

competency_groups.title: Analyses data using statistical concepts and probability
competency_groups.description: Analyses and interprets data using statistical concepts (such as measures of central tendency, standard deviations) and probability.
competencies: C-6.1 Applies measures of central tendencies, such as mean, median, and mode.

competency_groups.title: Analyses data using statistical concepts and probability
competency_groups.description: Analyses and interprets data using statistical concepts (such as measures of central tendency, standard deviations) and probability.
competencies: C-6.2 Applies concepts from probability to solve problems on the likelihood of everyday events.

competency_groups.title: Perceives axiomatic structure of Mathematics
competency_groups.description: Begins to perceive and appreciate the axiomatic and deductive structure of Mathematics.
competencies: C-7.1 Proves mathematical statements and carries out geometric constructions using stated assumptions, axioms, postulates, definitions, and mathematics vocabulary.

competency_groups.title: Perceives axiomatic structure of Mathematics
competency_groups.description: Begins to perceive and appreciate the axiomatic and deductive structure of Mathematics.
competencies: C-7.2 Visualises and appreciates geometric proofs for algebraic identities and other 'proofs without words'.

competency_groups.title: Builds skills: visualisation, optimisation, representation, modelling
competency_groups.description: Builds skills, such as visualisation, optimisation, representation, and mathematical modelling along with their application in daily life.
competencies: C-8.1 Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions.

competency_groups.title: Develops computational thinking
competency_groups.description: Develops computational thinking, i.e., deals with complex problems and is able to break them down into a series of simple problems that can then be solved by suitable procedures/algorithms.
competencies: C-9.1 Decomposes a problem into sub-problems.

competency_groups.title: Develops computational thinking
competency_groups.description: Develops computational thinking, i.e., deals with complex problems and is able to break them down into a series of simple problems that can then be solved by suitable procedures/algorithms.
competencies: C-9.2 Describes and analyses a sequence of instructions being followed.

competency_groups.title: Appreciates contributions of mathematicians from India and around the world
competency_groups.description: Knows and appreciates important contributions of mathematicians from India and around the world.
competencies: C-10.1 Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians (Indian and others) in the field of Mathematics.

competency_groups.title: Explores connections of Mathematics with other subjects
competency_groups.description: Explores connections of Mathematics with other subjects.
competencies: C-11.1 Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems or situations in multiple subjects across Science, Social Science, Visual Arts, Music, Vocational Education, and Sports.
